Netflix Connect · Executive Management Brief · Q1 2026
Netflix Connect
Project Command Centre
Social feature suite · 2026–2030 · $94.1M Investment · For Board Review
Overall: RED
First update: Nov 2024
Dec: Green Jan: Amber Feb: Red
Social feature suite to reduce annual subscriber churn from 2.25% to 1.91%, capturing 15% of addressable churn and generating $251M NPV across a 5-year investment period. Investment ask: $94.1M.
NPV Generated
$251M
↑ 4.1 × target
5yr Forecast
$94.1M
$1.1M over budget
YTD Spend
$14.8M
↑ $11.3M over budget
Churn Target
1.91%
From 2.25% baseline
Breakeven
1.5 yrs
From Q2 2027
Milestones
7 / 12
2 delayed
1 · Project Objective
Business Need
Netflix's annual churn of 2.25% costs an estimated $1.3B/yr in lost subscription revenue. As the streaming market matures and competition intensifies, content-led retention alone is insufficient. A non-content social engagement layer is required to create network stickiness and reduce switching costs.
Strategic Alignment
Subscriber Retention Non-content Monetisation Network Effects Social Discovery
Project Objective
To reduce Netflix's annual churn rate from 2.25% to ≤1.91% by Q4 2030 through a suite of social features — capturing 15% of addressable churn and delivering a net present value of $251M.
SMART Goal
Specific: Reduce effective churn via social features · Measurable: 2.25% → 1.91% · Achievable: Modelled on Spotify Jam benchmark · Relevant: Core retention KPI · Time-bound: Q4 2030
2 · Deliverables & Project Scope
In Scope — Deliverables
✓
Stage 1: Social User Interface
User profiles, friend discovery, privacy controls, recommendation feed
✓
Stage 1: Privacy & Compliance Infrastructure
GDPR-compliant data handling, consent flows, compliance team (4 FTE)
✓
Stage 2: Discussion Forums
Per-title community threads with AI + human content moderation
✓
Stage 2: Watch Party
Real-time synchronised viewing with live group chat (WebSocket)
✓
Stage 2: Netflix Wrapped
Annual personalised viewing summary, shareable social card
✓
Stage 3: Referral Model
1-month free incentive per successful referral; limited to launch window
Out of Scope — Exclusions
✕
Full-Scale User Generated Content
User-created videos outside Netflix titles excluded from all stages
✕
E-commerce Integration
No merchandise, partner commerce, or affiliate partnerships in scope
✕
Advanced Recommendation Algorithm ('For You Page')
Social signals may feed existing algorithm but new model is out of scope
✕
Additional Tier Pricing for Social Features
All social features available to existing subscribers at no extra cost
✕
Third-Party Streaming Platform Integration
Netflix-only — no cross-platform watch parties or shared watchlists
3 · Assumptions & Dependencies
Key Assumptions
📌
User base growth at 13.14% p.a.
Based on 5-year historical avg (Business of Apps). Assumed flat going forward.
📌
Existing churn remains at 2.25%
Without Netflix Connect intervention, quarterly churn held constant.
📌
Average monthly account value: $15.25
Weighted: 42% Basic ($17.99) + 40% Standard ($7.99) + 18% Premium ($24.99).
📌
Social features capture 15% of churn by Q4 2030
Conservative ramp: 2% captured in 3Q27, scaling to 15% by 4Q30.
📌
WACC discount rate: 7.17% (1.79% quarterly)
Source: AlphaSpread NFLX discount rate model. Used for NPV calculations.
📌
Referral programme drives 20% of captured churn
Referred users convert at 31–57% higher rate (academic research).
External Dependencies
🔗
GDPR Legal Sign-off
Jean Zang's compliance team + external legal counsel must approve privacy architecture before any user data collection. Target: Q2 2026.
Amber
🔗
Cloud Infrastructure Procurement
AWS / GCP contract negotiation for $500K/mo infrastructure commitment.
Green
🔗
Engineering Talent Acquisition
20 FTE engineers at peak. Competitive hiring market in SG/London/LA.
Green
🔗
Netflix Core Platform API Access
Social features depend on internal user viewing data APIs and recommendation engine hooks.
Green
🔗
Regional Localisation Partners
Cultural adaptation required for APAC, LATAM, and EMEA launch. Phased rollout planned.
Amber

6 · Milestones & Target Dates

7 · Technical Requirements
Infrastructure & Platform
☁
Cloud Infrastructure
AWS/GCP hybrid — $500K/mo for compute, storage, CDN, and global edge nodes. Scalable to 300M+ concurrent users.
🔄
Real-time Sync Engine
WebSocket-based for Watch Party — sub-200ms latency globally. Requires dedicated socket server fleet.
🗄
Social Graph Database
Neo4j graph DB for friend/follower relationships. Schema design: Q4 2026.
🤖
AI Content Moderation
Google Vertex AI pipeline for discussion forum and watch party chat. Human review escalation layer.
🔒
Privacy & Compliance Stack
GDPR-compliant consent management, data residency controls, right-to-erasure workflows. CCPA and PDPA ready.
Tech Stack
FRONTEND
React NativeTypeScriptReduxWebSockets
BACKEND
Node.jsGraphQLNeo4jRedisKafka
INFRASTRUCTURE
AWS EC2/EKSGCP Vertex AICloudFront CDNTerraform
DATA & COMPLIANCE
SnowflakedbtOneTrustDatadog
Team Headcount by Stage
Stage 1 (peak): 20 engineers · Stage 2: 15 engineers · Stage 3: 5 engineers · BAU: 4 compliance + 2 maintenance
8 · Limitations & Exclusions
Technical Limitations
⚠
Watch Party latency floor
Sub-200ms sync feasible in metro regions. Rural/emerging markets may experience 400–600ms delay — impacting UX for ~15% of global user base.
⚠
Moderation scalability
AI moderation accuracy ~94% for English content. Multilingual accuracy drops to ~82% — requires proportionally more human reviewers per region.
⚠
Friend graph cold-start
Social features require critical mass (~5% adoption) before network effects become self-sustaining. Early adoption KPI is key.
Regulatory Limitations
⚠
GDPR data residency
EU users' social data must remain within EU data centres. Separate infrastructure required, increasing cloud cost by est. $80K/mo.
⚠
China / Russia market
Social features cannot launch in restricted markets due to data sovereignty laws. Estimated 8M users excluded from social features.
⚠
Minor user protections
Under-18 accounts require parental consent flows and restricted social feature access under COPPA/GDPR-K regulations.
Commercial Limitations
⚠
No additional revenue tier
Social features are bundled with existing subscriptions. No premium social tier — benefits are retention-driven, not revenue-additive directly.
⚠
Referral programme time-limited
Referral model (Stage 3) only operates during initial feature launch window to prevent long-term CAC cannibalisation.
⚠
Churn model uncertainty
15% addressable churn capture is modelled on Spotify Jam data. Real Netflix social adoption may differ — sensitivity range: 10–20%.
9 · Customer Insights & Market Validation
★★★★★
"I'd watch way more if I could do it with friends without needing a third-party extension. Teleparty is fine but it's clunky — just build it into Netflix."
— Netflix subscriber, Focus Group A · Singapore · Age 24
★★★★★
"I cancel when there's nothing new to watch. But if my friends were on the platform discussing shows, I'd probably stick around even in the gaps between good content."
— Former subscriber, Churn Survey · USA · Age 31
★★★★★
"Spotify Wrapped made me feel seen. If Netflix did the same thing — showed me my year in shows — I'd never leave. That'd be something to share with everyone."
— Netflix subscriber, Survey Panel · UK · Age 27
Market Research Findings
📊
59% of Gen Z discover shows via online communities
Source: Deloitte Digital Media Trends 2025 — social discovery is the #1 driver for Gen Z content engagement.
📊
Teleparty: 10M+ users, zero Netflix revenue share
Demand for native social viewing is proven. Third-party capture represents an internalisation opportunity.
📊
Spotify: 21% MAU growth after Jam launch (Q3 2023)
Direct benchmark. Social features drove immediate user base expansion and reduced churn to 1.5%.
Competitive Benchmarks
🎵
Spotify — Churn: 1.5%
Social features (Jams, collaborative playlists, in-app messaging) credited with industry-lowest streaming churn.
Benchmark
🎮
Disney+ — Product Bundling
Hulu/Disney+/ESPN+ bundle reduced individual churn by creating multi-service stickiness.
Comparable
📦
Amazon Prime — Complementary Services
Prime Video bundled with delivery creates value beyond content — model for non-content retention.
Comparable

11 · Approval & Review Committee
Board Approvals
Review Schedule
📅
Monthly Steering Committee
Every 1st Monday · Ryan Wong presents progress to Steffi Tedjo and board observers. All RAG statuses reviewed.
📅
Quarterly Financial Review
Lucas Tan presents actuals vs budget to Finance Controller and CFO. Budget reforecast if variance >10%.
📅
Stage Gate Reviews
Full committee sign-off required before each stage investment is unlocked. Checkpoint process enforced in this dashboard.
📅
Annual Board Presentation
Full project status, NPV reforecast, and risk register presented to Netflix Board of Directors each December.
